reasoning: Capturing the city of Sumy (pre-war population 260,000) and its centrally located train station by September 30, 2025 is extremely unlikely. Sumy is located roughly 30km from the border, and despite some Russian advances in Sumy Oblast in early/mid-2025 aiming to create a buffer zone, the front has stabilized. The ISW assesses that seizing the regional capital is largely beyond the capabilities of the Russian forces currently there due to limited manpower and difficult, forested terrain. Historically in this war, Russia has needed many months to capture much smaller cities. Furthermore, there is no realistic prospect of Sumy being ceded to Russia in a negotiated settlement within the next two months. Given the tight two-month timeline and the lack of an active, deep breakthrough toward the city, the probability is near zero.
